sources: docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §6c (lines 147-153) — 'uniformly excluded — every honest node drops it'
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 47. marker-fold The rule (lines 974-975) — '§6c uniform-exclusion rule ... unchanged' [ratified]
docs/GLOSSARY.md data availability [candidate] — 'a batch whose bytes don't surface in time is excluded, never trusted'

inputs.attackFlagText: 'Bytes can't be produced by anyone' quantifies over an open server set — a pure predicate sees only the evidence presented to it, so evidence-gathering completeness is silently delegated to B3/adapters; an under-collecting adapter turns fail-closed exclusion into censorship of honest batches.

fixture.caseA_absentEvidence: witnessedServedEvidence empty/absent at an evaluationHeight past challengeDeadlineHeight -> reject (excluded)
fixture.caseB_insufficientEvidence: witnessedServedEvidence present but not matching the anchored (newRoot,batchSize) commitment, past the deadline -> reject (excluded)
fixture.failClosedProperty: at every evaluation height past the challenge deadline, absent/insufficient evidence yields ineligible — NEVER pending-include and NEVER fail-open
fixture.positiveCompanion: commitment-matching served bytes presented inside the window -> accept (eligible)

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (ratified marker-fold §6c uniform-exclusion + candidate DA §6c): the kernel-side fail-closed verdict — absent/insufficient served-evidence presented to the predicate yields ineligible, never trusted/provisional. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (B3/transport, NOT asserted): evidence-collection completeness and the under-collecting-adapter censorship concern (the flag's substance), plus the served-bytes witness FORMAT (B3). This vector asserts ONLY the no-evidence-in -> excluded-out predicate.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; status stays proposed pending reviewer sign-off.

expected.invariant: absent or insufficient served-evidence yields ineligible at every evaluation height past the challenge deadline; never a trusted or provisional include; the verdict fails closed
expected.rationale: Per the gate protocol, a flag purely about the kernel's fail-closed posture on absent evidence is derivable now even though the evidence-collection completeness lives in B3/adapters. The kernel-side verdict — absent/insufficient served-evidence presented to the predicate yields ineligible, never a trusted or provisional include — is grounded in ratified marker-fold and candidate §6c uniform-exclusion. The adapter-censorship substance is a B3/transport concern, but the testable kernel predicate (no evidence in → excluded out) is fully derivable.

sources: docs/spec/ONT_DATA_AVAILABILITY_AGREEMENT.md §5 (lines 102-123) [candidate] — delta only inserts; disjoint insertions commute; §4 (lines 91-100) — 'a hidden delta only fails to register its own names'
docs/spec/ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE.md Data Availability [candidate] — 'exclusion cannot take someone else's name; it only prevents the withheld data from counting'

inputs.attackFlagText: The guarantee leans on the insert-only/commutative merge fact (§5): if batched-path transitions ever admit non-insert operations inside batches, exclusion stops being self-contained — a scope coupling the batched-path area must hold.

fixture.batches: a set of accepted batches B0..Bn plus batch X
fixture.stateEquivalenceProperty: every other batch's names register identically to a world where X was never anchored -> accept
fixture.negativeCompanion: an exclusion implementation that perturbs an unrelated name's owner OR the root over the remaining leaves -> reject (fails)
fixture.crossAreaNote: assumes the insert-only-batches invariant, owned by the batched-path (B) area

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (candidate): DA §4/§5 (delta only inserts; disjoint insertions commute) + state-machine ('exclusion only prevents the withheld data from counting'). The insert-only-batches invariant is a CROSS-AREA dependency owned by the batched-path (B) area — this vector ASSUMES it, does not re-derive it; the flag's coupling caveat is an invariant B must preserve, not a missing-spec gap for D7's own verdict.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; status stays proposed pending reviewer sign-off.

expected.invariant: excluding batch X removes only X's own leaves; all other batches' names register identically to the as-if-X-never-anchored world; exclusion alters no other name's owner and no remaining-leaf root
expected.rationale: D7's verdict — excluding a batch removes only its own leaves and the resulting state equals the state as-if-that-batch-never-existed — is derivable today from candidate authority: DA agreement §4/§5 establish insert-only/commutative merge, and the state-machine pins that exclusion 'only prevents the withheld data from counting'. The flag is a scope-coupling caveat (it holds only while batched-path transitions stay insert-only), which is an invariant the batched-path (B) area must preserve, not a missing-spec gap for D7's own verdict. The state-equivalence property is testable now.
